title: The No-Nonsense Guide to Building a Crypto Store on GitHub Pages — No PayPal Required
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: From zero to live crypto store in one afternoon. GitHub Pages hosting, Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, NOWPayments integration.

content.text: You're a digital creator in Nigeria, and you've finally finished your ebook on cryptocurrency trading. But when you try to sell it on platforms like Gumroad or Payhip, you're blocked because of PayPal's restrictions on your country. You need a way to sell your digital products without getting stuck in the middle of bureaucratic red tape. Let's build a crypto store on GitHub Pages with NOWPayments and Core Shop Engine, a no-PayPal-required solution that's perfect for creators like you.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

The beauty of using GitHub Pages is that it's a static site generator that doesn't require any server-side code or infrastructure. You can host your store for free, without worrying about maintenance or scalability. And with NOWPayments, you can accept a wide range of cryptocurrencies, including Bitcoin, Ethereum, and stablecoins like USDT. This setup is perfect for creators who need a fast and reliable way to sell their digital products, without getting bogged down in complex payment processing or technical details.
What You Need Before You Start

Before you begin, make sure you have a GitHub account and a basic understanding of HTML and CSS. You'll also need to install Node.js and npm on your computer, as well as the Core Shop Engine and Web3 Wallet Connect plugins. Don't worry if these terms sound unfamiliar – we'll walk through each step in detail. The most important thing is to have a digital product ready to sell, such as an ebook or a course.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

With Core Shop Engine, you can create a payment pipeline that's fully automated and based on blockchain transactions. This means that buyers can pay with their preferred cryptocurrency, and you'll receive the payment in a matter of minutes. To set this up, you'll need to create a NOWPayments account and connect it to your Core Shop Engine instance. The Web3 Wallet Connect plugin will help buyers pay with their preferred cryptocurrency, and generate a multi-chain QR code for easy payment.
Accepting cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and Ethereum can expand your store's customer base and increase average order values.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

Once you've set up your payment and delivery pipeline, it's time to go live and start selling your digital products. To do this, you'll need to create a GitHub Pages repository and upload your store's code. You can customize the design and layout of your store using HTML and CSS, and add new products and payment options as needed. When a buyer makes a purchase, the Core Shop Engine plugin will automatically deliver the product and send a receipt to the buyer's email address.
Scaling Once the System Is Running
How do I build a digital store that accepts crypto payments?

The cheapest way to set up a digital product store is to use GitHub Pages and Core Shop Engine, a no-PayPal-required solution that's perfect for creators in restricted countries. With this setup, you can accept a wide range of cryptocurrencies and deliver products to buyers automatically. The total cost is around $50, including the Web3 Wallet Connect plugin, which is a small price to pay for the freedom to sell your digital products without restrictions.
The Core Shop Engine plugin can automate payment processing and delivery for a seamless customer experience.
Related: How Creators Like You Are Getting Paid

If you're looking for more ways to monetize your digital products, you might also want to check out our other tutorials on using GitHub Pages for e-commerce, integrating Web3 Wallet Connect with other blockchain-based payment services, or setting up a shop using the Core Shop Engine plugin. You can also visit our shop at https://unchained-commerce.github.io/Shop/index.html to see how it works in practice.
Your Action Checklist

To set up a no-PayPal-required crypto store on GitHub Pages, follow these steps: Create a GitHub account and install Node.js and npm on your computer. Install the Core Shop Engine and Web3 Wallet Connect plugins, and create a NOWPayments account. Set up your payment and delivery pipeline using Core Shop Engine, and connect it to your NOWPayments account. Create a GitHub Pages repository and upload your store's code, and customize the design and layout using HTML and CSS. Add new products and payment options as needed, and optimize your store's performance and user experience continuously.
Web3 Wallet Connect can integrate your store with other blockchain-based payment services for even more flexibility.
Web3 Wallet Connect — $19.99. Frontend wallet integration with multi-chain QR generation and live price feeds. Let buyers pay in BTC, ETH, USDT, SOL, and 300 more coins.
